author: Dr. Lizard
title: How is hyponatremia classified based on extracellular fluid volume status for diagnosis?
answer: Hyponatremia is classified as hypovolemic, euvolemic, or hypervolemic based on extracellular fluid volume assessment.
detail: Volume status assessment provides the most useful classification because serum sodium alone is insufficient.
Hypovolemic hyponatremia results from decreased extracellular fluid volume with sodium and water losses.
Euvolemic hyponatremia occurs with normal extracellular fluid volume, commonly from SIAD or primary polydipsia.
Hypervolemic hyponatremia involves increased extracellular fluid volume, seen in heart failure, cirrhosis, or nephrotic syndrome.
releaseNote: Hyponatremia can be classified into three types based on extracellular fluid volume status: hypovolemic, euvolemic, and hypervolemic, each with distinct underlying causes and characteristics.
